channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Deputy President Mashatile speaks at Nepad 25th anniversary business breakfast in Cape Town
item.description: On Thursday, Deputy President Paul Mashatile delivered the keynote address at the Nepad@25 business breakfast held in Cape Town. The event was organised by the African Union Development Agency in coll...

item.title: TeamPCP Cybercrime Group Conducts Large-Scale Supply Chain Attacks on Open Source Software
item.description: TeamPCP, a cybercriminal group, has escalated software supply chain attacks to an unprecedented level, compromising hundreds of open source tools used by developers worldwide. Their latest target was ...

item.title: Form Three Student in Bomet Hospitalised after Classmate Stabbing
item.description: In Bomet County, a 17-year-old Form Three student at Chemalal AIC Secondary School in Konoin is currently fighting for her life at Litein Level Four Hospital following a stabbing incident. The suspect...

item.title: Guide to Choosing Effective Promotional Gifts for Corporate and Tech Events in 2026
item.description: For brands wey wan make dem stand out for conferences and corporate events in 2026, giving the right promotional gifts still dey very important. Back in the day, brands like Golden Morn, Ovaltine, Mil...

item.title: China's Diplomatic Meetings Highlight Contrasting Relations with U.S. and Russia
item.description: Chinese leader Xi Jinping recently held consecutive meetings with U.S. President Donald Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin in Beijing. While both summits featured formalities such as handshake...

item.title: Iringa to Host Competitive Mkwawa Water Rally with Top Drivers
item.description: Dar es Salaam – More than 15 skilled drivers are set to take part in the Mkwawa Pure Drinking Water Rally, happening early next month in Iringa Region. According to Iringa Region Motorsport chairman R...

item.title: PCSO Reports No Jackpot Winners in Major Lotto Draws for May 21, 2026
item.description: The Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) announced that there were no winners for the major lotto jackpot draws conducted on Thursday, May 21, 2026. In the Superlotto 6/49 draw, which had a ja...

item.title: NYSC and NIMC collaborate to simplify registration and verification for corps members
item.description: The National Youth Service Corps (NYSC) don join hand with National Identity Management Commission (NIMC) to make life easier for prospective corps members wey dey all over Nigeria. According to NYSC ...

item.title: Leucine Enhances Mitochondrial Efficiency and Cellular Energy Production
item.description: Researchers at the University of Cologne have identified the essential amino acid leucine as a key regulator of mitochondrial energy production. Mitochondria, the cell's energy powerhouses, rely on sp...

item.title: PMD warns of intense heatwave in Pakistan during Eid holidays
item.description: The Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) has issued a warning that moderate to severe heatwave conditions are likely to affect large parts of the country during the Eid ul Adha period, from May 25...

item.title: Trump's Endorsement of Ken Paxton Could Risk Republican Senate Seat in Texas
item.description: Fox News chief political analyst Brit Hume warned that former President Donald Trump's endorsement of Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton in the Republican Senate runoff may inadvertently benefit Democr...

item.title: Kipkenyo MCA Returns to School After 50 Years, Scores KCSE D- and Inspires Community
item.description: David Letting, the MCA for Kipkenyo in Uasin Gishu County, popularly known as Kokwas, has captured the hearts of his constituents with his inspiring journey back to education. Dropping out of school i...

item.title: Mona Shares Valuable Advice to Bubbles and Charlie in Episode 47 of The Alibi
item.description: In Episode 47 of the popular Philippine TV series 'The Alibi,' the character Mona delivers insightful guidance to her friends Bubbles and Charlie, addressing the challenges they are currently facing. ...

item.title: Highest Number of Climbers Reach Mount Everest in One Day from Nepal
item.description: On Wednesday, May 20, 2026, a record 274 climbers reached the summit of Mount Everest from the Nepali side, marking the highest number of people to climb the world’s tallest peak in a single day from ...

item.title: Jane Fonda’s Breakout Role in ‘Walk on the Wild Side’ (1962)
item.description: In the 1962 film 'Walk on the Wild Side,' Jane Fonda delivered a powerful early performance as Kitty Twist, a cunning and street-smart drifter who becomes involved in the darker aspects of a New Orlea...

item.title: UN Prosecutor Concludes Talks on Genocide Justice in Rwanda
item.description: The chief prosecutor of the United Nations International Residual Mechanism for Criminal Tribunals, Serge Brammertz, has finished his official visit to Rwanda. During this visit, he held high-level me...

item.title: Russia Offers Support Amid Ebola Outbreak in Uganda and DRC
item.description: Russia has expressed deep concern over the recent Ebola outbreak affecting Uganda and the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC). Speaking on Thursday, Maria Zakharova, spokesperson for the Russian Fo...

item.title: Tensions in Durban as Police Intervene in Clashes Between Foreign Nationals and Anti-Immigrant Groups
item.description: On Thursday, outside the Diakonia Centre in Durban, tensions flared between foreign nationals seeking refuge and members of anti-immigrant groups, including March and March. The dispute reportedly beg...

item.title: Challenges in Managing the Bundibugyo Ebola Outbreak in the DRC
item.description: A rare strain of Ebola, known as Bundibugyo, is spreading in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), a region affected by conflict and displacement. Since April, over 130 deaths and more than ...

item.title: Supreme Court of Canada to rule on B.C. Indigenous mineral claims dispute
item.description: The Supreme Court of Canada is set to determine whether it will hear British Columbia’s appeal regarding a significant legal dispute over mineral claims and Indigenous rights. In December, the B.C. Co...
